meta.purpose: Leonoria linguistic system — languages, scripts, speakers, complexity, and cultural context
meta.scope: All 13 sub-races plus common trade tongues

common_tongues.name: The Common Tongue
common_tongues.origin: Evolved from early Erdan dialects, simplified over centuries of cross-race trade in the Midlands.
common_tongues.geographic_range: The Midlands, The Gleam Havens, The Outer Steppes, trade routes everywhere
common_tongues.notes: The default shared language on Leonoria. Nearly everyone who trades or travels speaks at least a basic form of it. The Thyraen and Velkaar are notable exceptions.

languages.name: Aelthari
languages.own_name_of_language: Aelthari — 'the voice of the inner light'
languages.origin: One of the oldest living languages on Leonoria. Developed in isolation within The Sanctuary Lands over millennia. Has never been simplified.
languages.geographic_range: The Sanctuary Lands exclusively. Rarely heard outside its borders.
languages.notes: Tonal and context-heavy. The same phrase can carry different meaning depending on the speaker's emotional state, time of day, and the health of surrounding plant life. Outsiders who attempt it are gently tolerated. The Aelthiryn and Talarin share root vocabulary.

languages.name: Saerathi
languages.script: Grey Script (geometric, angular, highly precise — no ambiguous letterforms)
languages.origin: Developed alongside Saereth architecture and mathematical philosophy. Designed to eliminate ambiguity.
languages.geographic_range: The Gleam Havens primarily. Used in scholarly and mercantile correspondence throughout coastal regions.
languages.notes: Has no idioms. Metaphors are considered imprecise and are avoided. Saereth Greys consider the Common Tongue distressingly vague. The language has an elaborate tense system covering not just when something happened but how certain the speaker is that it happened.

languages.name: Vel'khari
languages.also_called: Void-speech, The Dark Tongue, The Whisper
languages.script: Void Script (written in near-invisible ink visible only in darkness or under darkvoid light)
languages.origin: Evolved deep underground, shaped by centuries in total darkness. Contains sounds and resonances that affect the nervous system of light-sensitive creatures.
languages.geographic_range: The Forgotten Kingdom. Fragments appear in some Boglands ruins.
languages.notes: Known to cause mild disorientation in non-Velkaar listeners when spoken in its full ritual form. The Velkaar have multiple registers: a surface register for interaction with Dornak and others, and a deep register restricted to internal use and ritual. The deep register is never written in Common Script.

languages.name: Thyraen
languages.script: Crystal Script (carved into ice or bone; characters shatter if improperly formed)
languages.origin: Formed from the ritualistic invocations used to bind the first cold elementals. The language is partly functional: correct pronunciation of certain phrases has demonstrable environmental effects.
languages.geographic_range: The Eternal Winds exclusively. The Thyraen do not teach it to outsiders.
languages.notes: Contains a class of words with no translation — concepts related to stillness, cold, and waiting that do not exist in warmer cultures. Outsiders who learn fragments are viewed with suspicion rather than admiration.

languages.name: Erdan
languages.script: Erdan Script (the basis of Common Script)
languages.origin: The oldest of the human folk languages. Became the basis of Common Trade Tongue after Erdan merchants dominated Midlands trade routes.
languages.geographic_range: The Midlands, spreading along all major trade routes
languages.notes: Rich in agricultural and geographical terms. Has more words for types of soil, weather, and river conditions than any other language on Leonoria. The Skarn and Zai'Keth both adopted elements of Erdan when forced to deal with Midlands trade.

languages.name: Skarnvok
languages.script: Skarn Runic (carved marks, used for permanence; does not lend itself to quick writing)
languages.origin: Developed along the cold northern borders where the Midlands meets the Dark Forests and Eternal Winds. Shaped by practical survival — short words, clear meaning, no wasted breath.
languages.geographic_range: Northern Midlands, Dark Forest edges, scattered through cold regions
languages.notes: Has no word for 'unnecessary'. Skarn speakers using Common sometimes seem blunt or rude — they are simply continuing the linguistic habit of stripping filler. Has strong borrowings from Gruthen and Thyraen in its northern dialect.

languages.name: Zai
languages.script: Zai Mark System (symbols tied to routes and landmarks; highly mobile, not used for literature)
languages.own_name_of_language: Zai — 'the tongue of the open path'
languages.origin: Developed across the Outer Steppes by nomadic trade and raiding cultures. Highly adaptable — absorbs words from every language the Zai'Keth encounter along their routes.
languages.geographic_range: The Outer Steppes, The Blinding Lands edges, wherever Zai'Keth routes pass
languages.notes: Has an unusually large vocabulary for navigation, weather prediction, and horse-rearing. Its mark system is not a true writing system — it is a trail notation system for path-finding. Zai'Keth poetry is entirely oral.

languages.name: Gruth
languages.script: None (oral tradition; some elder marks carved into trees, but not a formal script)
languages.origin: Developed in the deep Dark Forests alongside centuries of bark-and-root material culture. Has many words for fungi, root types, and forest floor conditions.
languages.notes: Spoken quietly. Loud speech in Gruth is considered threatening or panicked. Has a complex honor-register system based on age and role in the community. Outsiders often fail to learn the honor-registers and accidentally speak to Gruthen elders as children.

languages.name: Krotha
languages.also_called: Blood Speech, Crusher's Tongue, The Loud Tongue
languages.script: Bone Marks (carved into trophies and weapons; records kills and lineage)
languages.origin: The simplest of all Leonorian languages by grammar, but with an enormous vocabulary for combat, prey, lineage, and hierarchy. Everything unrelated to survival or domination has no word.
languages.notes: Volume is meaningful in Krotha — louder generally means higher social position in context. The concept of 'asking' does not exist grammatically; requests are phrased as commands with tone modifiers. Has no word for mercy. The Krothan Bone Count (carved numbers of confirmed kills) is technically a writing system, but records only numbers.

languages.name: Talarin Root Speech
languages.script: Root Inscription (patterns that grow into wood over time; readable only with Athropium awareness)
languages.own_name_of_language: No spoken name — identified by sound and growth-pattern, not label
languages.origin: Shares ancient roots with Aelthari but diverged as the Talarin became physically bound to The Sanctuary Lands. Part of the language is spoken, part is expressed through changes in growth around the speaker.
languages.notes: Non-Athropium users can only hear the spoken half of conversations. The other half — expressed through root movement, bark vibration, and new growth — is completely inaccessible without Materium awareness. The Aelthiryn can hear both halves.

languages.name: Dornakk
languages.script: Chisel Script (carved into stone; highly durable; the Dornak do not consider text written on anything else to be 'real')
languages.own_name_of_language: Dornakk — 'the tongue of the deep stone'
languages.origin: Developed underground across millennia of mining, building, and deep-citadel culture. Grammar reflects the layered, structured thinking of the Dornak — everything has a category, a sub-category, and a material classification.
languages.geographic_range: The Forgotten Kingdom (Dornak sections), deep mines and tunnels throughout Leonoria
languages.notes: Has an extremely precise material vocabulary — more words for types of stone, ore, and mineral formation than any other language. Time is measured differently: the Dornak count in 'depth-years', related to how deep underground a community lives. The Dornak and Velkaar co-exist uneasily underground; many Dornak speak enough Vel'khari to trade but resent doing so.

languages.name: Urv
languages.script: Spore Script (patterns left in dried fungal spore; degrades quickly; not considered permanent)
languages.own_name_of_language: Urv — 'the tongue of the deep wet'
languages.origin: Developed in the deepest bogs, where sound carries strangely through water and mist. Many phonemes are produced with the throat partially closed — a sound pattern that is difficult for non-Urveth to reproduce without practice.
languages.notes: Rich in chemical and biological vocabulary. Has detailed terminology for types of venom, decay, and fermentation. The Urveth consider this their practical knowledge preserved in language. Has no concept of 'clean' — the closest word translates to 'temporarily without useful organisms'.

languages.name: Ul'ka
languages.script: Scar Script (burned or scarred into surfaces; mirrors the physical transformation the Ul'Karash experience)
languages.origin: A hybrid language born from exile. The Ul'Karash ancestors were cast out from multiple parent cultures; their language blended elements of whatever tongues they carried into the Badlands with new words born from the transformative environment.
languages.geographic_range: The Badlands, some Forgotten Kingdom edges
languages.notes: The only language on Leonoria that actively incorporates words from multiple parent tongues as part of its core vocabulary — not as loanwords, but as foundational elements. Linguists consider it a natural creole. The Ul'Karash themselves consider it proof that transformation creates something new rather than something lesser.
